TextENGNew: We walked down the single road with the charcoal suicide car behind us.
changeReason: New translation adds 'charcoal' detail from 練炭 and corrects 'narrow road' to 'single road' matching 一本道

TextENGNew: "This is..."
Before long, we arrived back on a country road.
Beyond the ridgeline, pure white clouds floated in the sky. Before us stretched verdant greenery. Weathered houses lined the road, which continued onward in a single line.
changeReason: Changes cloud from singular to plural and corrects road description to 'single line' matching 一本道

TextENGNew: "...A village."
"What are you, half asleep?@k@vS10/43/410400682. It's been the same village from the shrine to here — Hinamizawa village."
changeReason: Corrects 'talking in your sleep' to 'half asleep' matching 寝ぼけた, and adds 'village' matching 雛見沢村

TextENGNew: Saying that, Sonozaki-san strode ahead without stopping.
The decaying wooden houses were draped in cobwebs... windows were shattered, glass scattered everywhere.
changeReason: Corrects singular 'house' to plural 'houses' matching the Japanese 家屋 (generic/plural)

TextENGNew: "...Sigh.@k@vS10/62/410101599. Staring at mountains and trees, you don't really notice. ...But when you see the houses, it really hits home that this is an abandoned village."
Suddenly, I remembered that I came here for work... But I was in no mood to be taking pictures.
After everything that's happened here, I couldn't bring myself to think about journalism.
That moment, Otobe suddenly stopped in his tracks. He was staring at a large white mansion up ahead.
"Whoa...@k@vS10/41/410200916. I was just thinking even though these houses are really old, there's one that doesn't look so old..."
"Whoa.@k@vS10/62/410101601. Now that's a humongous house."
"I feel like someone rich must've lived there."
Sonozaki-san stopped beside us, then gently gazed at the mansion.
It seemed like she had completely tuned out our existence... like she was remembering something.
"H-hey! What's wrong?@k@vS10/62/410101603. You stopped all of a sudden..."
Even though I called out to her, Sonozaki-san didn't answer.
Her eyes were gazing far off in the distance...
Is this... a dream? An illusion?
Or maybe...?!
"Hurry, let's go..."
Someone dressed in a school uniform slipped by my side, running like the wind. It was "him."
"Damn it, we're late!!@k@vS10/01/411000023. We need to move, fast!"
"W-wait up, Keiichi-kuuun!!"
Then, a girl dressed in a skirt ran by my other side. It was "her."
"Who's gonna get there first? Is it a race?@k@vS10/02/411100003. Whoever gets to their seat first wins!"
"All right, I was hoping you'd say that!@k@vS10/01/411000025. I've got the strength of a man!! Don't underestimate me!!"
The two of them gently looked back at me and waved.
They were bursting with energy... with huge smiles on their faces.
"What's wrong, Mion?!"
"Mii-chan, hurry up!!"
"Rena... Kei-chan..."
Right... that's right.
Wait for me, Kei-chan, Rena.
I'll be there soon... because I'm heading there... right away...
"Sonozaki-san...?"

Sonozaki-san let out a quick sigh before coming back to her senses. She had been gazing at that mansion with a nostalgic look in her eyes for a while. Then, she gently wiped the corner of her eye and turned away.
"...Are you all right?"
"Yeah. Pardon me for stopping.@k@vS10/43/410400687. ...Let's go."
Then, Sonozaki-san took off walking at a quick pace.
She said she was a resident of this village. If that's the truth... then maybe she has some precious memories of the person who lived in that house...?
"...Well, whatever."
Honestly, I was interested to learn more... but this wasn't the time.
Sonozaki-san was walking at such a brisk pace I had a hard enough time keeping up with her, let alone time to think idle thoughts.
Shortly afterwards, I began to see a chain link fence stretched along the shoulder of the road.
The fence was tall, and the sharp, spear-like tips were folded toward this side. Beyond that, some rusty tattered barbed wire was wrapped around.
There were signs attached to the fence here and there.
The signs were battered from exposure to the elements, so the letters were faded and hardly legible.
I could just barely make out the characters for "no trespassing" and "no entry." Apparently, they wanted to make it abundantly clear this area was off limits.
"Mion-san... all of this... is your house...?"
"I wouldn't describe it as a house so much as property.@k@vS10/43/410400689. It's what they call a private estate."
"Everything... inside there... is your yard...?"
"Not at all. It's just property.@k@vS10/43/410400691. There's nothing out there but mountains."
Overcome with curiosity, Otobe restlessly scanned our surroundings. ...I'd be snapping photos of the entire area if not for our current situation.
...Unfortunate.
Before long, a magnificent fence came into view that seemed to encircle the entire mansion.
This was the headquarters of the entire Sonozaki clan, one of the Three Great Families who controlled Hinamizawa in the old days.
Everything surrounding the mansion boasted an intimidating facade, unashamed to flaunt the Sonozaki name.
Mechanical objects that almost looked like toys were plastered here and there on the wall.
Perhaps those were the remnants of security cameras installed here in the past...?
Eventually... we reached a gate with a massive set of double doors that looked like a shrine or something.
"I'd expect no less from the Three Families...@k@vS10/62/410101608. That's a rather impressive gate."
The gate was decaying from exposure to the wind and rain, and spider webs were littered all around it.
However... the spider webs stretched across the edge of one of the doors appeared unnaturally broken.
Most likely, proof that other people had been coming or going through here just a little while ago...
After examining the spider webs in disarray, Sonozaki-san leaned forward and lightly peered through the gap between the doors.
Sonozaki-san let out a telling sigh before calling back to Otobe and me.
She began to say "Ready?" in a hushed voice like she was trying to warn us.

"The secret passage is deep in the Sonozaki forest.@k@vS10/43/410400695. Go past the garden. The entrance is in an underground storeroom further back.@k@vS10/43/410400696. But... getting there won't be easy."
"Our guests from earlier... are waiting for us."
Sonozaki-san gestured forward with her chin. I looked through the gap in the door as she indicated... and couldn't help but raise my voice.
There were a dozen or so men dressed in black suits, all standing in a line and facing this direction.
As if... they were just waiting for us to open the gate.
"Wh... what should we do...?!"
"It can't be helped. We just have to do it."
"D-d-d-d-d-do it...?!"
"Relax, I can fight them alone.@k@vS10/43/410400700. You guys should wait here."
Is Sonozaki-san planning to take them all down on her own...?
Isn't that totally reckless...?
"However... after I deal with them, you'll be on your own to reach the secret passage.@k@vS10/43/410400702. Because I need to go get that bell."
"Then... I'll go with you!"
"Don't be absurd.@k@vS10/43/410400704. Any civilian walking into the main house unarmed will be slaughtered in an instant."
"But... I have to do something, for your sake, Mion-san...!"
Otobe desperately pleaded with her.
Sonozaki-san looked into his eyes and smiled bitterly.

"...I understand why you'd want to put your life on the line.@k@vS10/43/410400706. However... you still have a full life ahead of you.@k@vS10/43/410400707. ...This is my problem,@k@vS10/43/410400708. so I'll settle it on my own."
"...Mion-san, I'm glad I met you.@k@vS10/41/410200926. Before meeting you, I never could have spoken about my hardships. It feels like I've been reborn."
"...So please, promise me this.@k@vS10/41/410200928. ...Please.@k@vS10/41/410200929. You must not, under any circumstances... throw your life away!"
Otobe gazed at Sonozaki-san with a serious expression. Then, she responded to his desperate plea with a shrug of her shoulders and a bitter smile.
"...All right, I promise.@k@vS10/43/410400710. Anyway, you guys need to advance during the gap I open for you."
"...What about you, Mion-san?"
"I'm heading to the back of the main house.@k@vS10/43/410400712. ...The bell should be behind a hanging scroll.@k@vS10/43/410400713. The lock is a decorated scabbard sitting in an alcove."
"The lock is... a scabbard...?"
"That's right, there's a mechanism that activates if the "Tamahajiki sword" is inserted there."
"So, if you put the sword in that particular sheath, a door or something will open leading to the bell...?"
"That's right, there should be a door hidden behind the scroll.@k@vS10/43/410400716. The bell is stored inside there."

"Once I get it open, I'll grab the bell and head for the underground storeroom in the forest.@k@vS10/43/410400718. There's a well hidden inside that connects to an underground passage.@k@vS10/43/410400719. We'll met up there."
"Got it...!"
Otobe responded vigorously.
His rejoicing made it all the way through to my heart.
Otobe was thrilled that Sonozaki-san was willing to share the details of her plan with us.
This is what it's like to earn someone's trust...
"Arakawa."
"Huh? Ah, yes?!"
"You and Otobe need to head to the secret passage together.@k@vS10/43/410400722. I'll catch up later."
Sonozaki-san carefully looked over both our faces.
For a moment, her pupils opened wide and her eyes darkened.
Probably concerned about leaving this world before accomplishing her goal in life.
When the light returned to her eyes, Sonozaki-san stiffened her lip.
"Alright... let's go!!"
Sonozaki-san lifted the hem of her kimono and vigorously kicked the door.
*wham*
"...Hmph."
As soon as the door came open, the men in black reached for their guns and swords all at once.
A sharp-eyed man with gray hair was standing in the middle of the group.

"Hoh... rather bold of you to issue a challenge knowing we were here.@k@vS10/00/410800002. I'd expect no less from my predecessor.@k@vS10/00/410800003. Former head of the Sonozaki family... Mion Sonozaki."
"Hah.@k@vS10/43/410400726. Big mouth from someone who had to bring his lackeys with him,@k@vS10/43/410400727. ...Mifune!"
The man she called Mifune twisted the edge of his mouth into a thin smile.

"I assume my mother's shooting was your handiwork?@k@vS10/43/410400729. You were loyal to the previous family head, so I can't believe you'd betray the Sonozaki family.@k@vS10/43/410400730. You've sure got guts, waltzing into the Sonozaki family's doorstep like it's nothing...!"

"Allow me to counter that argument. You have no place calling me a traitor.@k@vS10/00/410800005. I was the one who advised that a woman like you didn't have what it took to keep a firm grip on the reins of the Sonozaki group."
Mifune's expression didn't change at all as he declared that. Sonozaki-san kept her sharp, bloodthirsty gaze firmly focused on him.

"Originally, I was the designated family head and next in line to lead the Sonozaki group, but you were always the most strongly opposed.@k@vS10/43/410400732. You always complained I was young and inexperienced..."

"I heard from Kasai... You were dabbling in smuggling operations behind my father's back, weren't you?@k@vS10/43/410400734. In order to keep it from being exposed... and to expand that business... you killed Mom and Dad...!!"
"...I don't know which lackey you heard that from, but it's a false accusation.@k@vS10/00/410800007. I've been doing everything in my power to expand the profits and influence of the group."
"Your father was jealous and tried to take it all for himself...@k@vS10/00/410800009. In the end, the weight of the Sonozaki Group's name was too heavy a burden for people like you."
"Whether the burden is too much to bear or not... is not something you could hope to determine.@k@vS10/43/410400736. How can people with eyes clouded by greed pass judgment?"
"Heh...@k@vS10/00/410800011. It's in your best interests to shut that mouth of yours right now.@k@vS10/00/410800012. ...It's time to get down to business.@k@vS10/00/410800013. Hand over the bell."
Mifune stretched out his hand and lifted the sleeve of his kimono while approaching Sonozaki-san, step by step.
At the same time, the men in black slowly closed the distance between us.

"...Even I'm not heartless enough to raise a hand against the daughter of a man I served.@k@vS10/00/410800015. If you tell me where it is, I'll spare your life.@k@vS10/00/410800016. ...What do you say?"
"Don't make me laugh.@k@vS10/43/410400738. You must already know, I'd never hand it over so easily.@k@vS10/43/410400739. ...This worthless chatter is simply a waste of both our time."
Mifune let out a strange cackle, but his eyes weren't laughing at all.
Then, he turned to Otobe and I with eyes like a predator stalking its prey...
Otobe's shoulders sank like he had taken a physical blow.
...What are you doing, Otobe?!
If you lose your resolve now, you won't be any help to Sonozaki-san. You'll just get in the way!
"Arakawa-san..."
Otobe gently grabbed my arm while calling out to me in a hushed voice.
I shook off Otobe's arm and resolutely stared back at Mifune.
"Don't you... make light of Sonozaki-san."
"...Kill them."
On that provocation... Mifune lightly twitched his lips.
And the next moment, the men in black all began to move at once...!!
"Wait, what...?!@k@vS10/62/410101618. No way, did my flippant remark just trigger the battle?!"
"Arakawa-san, what are you doing?!@k@vS10/41/410200936. Wah?!"
Otobe grabbed my hand and pulled me behind the door.
Immediately after that, the men in black fired two bullets back to back.
H-hold on! You gotta be kidding me!
These guys don't hesitate for a moment to attack civilians?!
What was all that bullcrap about not being heartless?!
Just then,
"Gwah?!"
"Don't waste your bullets on amateurs!"
Sonozaki-san slashed her still-sheathed sword toward the man's wrist, knocking the gun clean out of his hand.
The man looked back, then pulled a knife out of his pocket. But before he could do anything more, she swung her katana into the man's neck.
"Gah?!"
In just two seconds, one man had already collapsed to the ground.
Then, as the other men looked on and prepared themselves... Sonozaki-san pulled the string on her sword bag and slowly pulled the sheathed sword from within.
"Bring it on.@k@vS10/43/410400742. If anyone would like a taste, I'm happy to serve you."
"Mion-sa...!"
"Don't come any closer!"
Otobe began to step out from behind the door, but stopped the moment he heard Sonozaki-san's commanding voice.
That moment, she pulled the Tamahajiki sword from its scabbard and the blade glistened in the sunlight.
She stood in an unconventional stance with the sword held underhanded, which seemed to leave many openings.
But she gazed forward with a piercing glare... far too terrifying to easily take advantage of the opening.
"I can't be civil here.@k@vS10/43/410400745. If you don't want to die, don't come any closer!!"
...She was filled to the brim with overwhelming determination.
How is she able to hide all that energy in that slender body of hers...?!
"Damn you...!"
"Bitch!!"
Undaunted, the men all leapt toward Sonozaki-san at once. However,
"...Too slow!!"
"Geh?!"
"Ugh!!"
The men collapsed to their knees, then down to the ground.
...Blood was pouring out everywhere I looked. It was clear as day that this was a battlefield.
But their fighting spirit gradually waned after a few skillful blows from steel with the speed and sharpness to cut through bone.
"Take that!"
As the men in black pressed on armed with knives, Sonozaki-san let loose with her katana. She took one down with a sweep of her leg, then sliced into his stomach...!
"Gah?!"
There were at least 10 men in black... but in the blink of an eye, they were falling to the ground one by one.
Some were still twitching on the ground, unable to rise to their feet.
Of course they didn't bother moving... The shock from those sword attacks and the fear of Sonozaki-san would make it impossible to rise up...!!
They were defeated in an instant. The people who fell to the ground in a flash immediately realized the difference in their ability.
Amazing... Absolutely amazing...!
This many opponents really isn't a problem at all for Sonozaki-san...?
She had that much spirit.
In their clash of spirit and energy, Sonozaki-san claimed total victory.
I'd expect no less... from the future head of the Sonozaki family.
I couldn't take my eyes off her dance of swords.
And two other people gazed at her with eyes just like mine.
Otobe, and the other man over there... Mifune.

"...It's certainly bold of you to fight your way through so many enemies.@k@vS10/00/410800019. With calm, precise attacks that unquestionably cut off their movement, yet don't deprive them of their lives.@k@vS10/00/410800020. ...As expected of Mion Sonozaki."
"...Hmph.@k@vS10/43/410400749. If you're that impressed, why not come closer?@k@vS10/43/410400750. Instead of standing petrified over there like a scarecrow?!"

"Don't be absurd.@k@vS10/00/410800022. ...However, it is one of my principles to show respect for outstanding opponents, even enemies.@k@vS10/00/410800023. I'll take you up on that if the opportunity arises."
"Hah!@k@vS10/43/410400752. That opportunity might come knocking before you know it...!!"
After saying that, Sonozaki-san took a look around.
Among the men in black... only one was still standing.
The man called Mifune must not have expected her to have taken care of so many minions all on her own. His eyes were open wide in shock.
And on top of that... even after such an extraordinary battle, she wasn't even out of breath.
"Huh?"
"He ran off. Coward."
Mifune spat out in disgust, then turned and ran. After that, the one remaining man in black approached Sonozaki-san with his teeth chattering, armed with a wooden sword.
"...Guess this means you're alone, huh?"
Sonozaki-san held her sword toward the man in black. Then,
"...I... I... I'm sorry... I'm really sorry!"
He must've realized what we already knew. He tossed his wooden sword aside and dropped down to his knees, rubbing his forehead in the ground.
He gave in all too soon... and brought this confrontation to a miserable end.
"Please forgive me! Please...!@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Yhead1004. I'm begging you!"
"...What are you trying to pull?"

"I was an idiot.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Yhead1006. It was shallow of me to turn my blade on Mion Sonozaki... the true head of the family.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Yhead1007. It was inexcusable, but I'm begging you. Have mercy!@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Yhead1008. Please, forgive me!"
Sonozaki-san looked down at the man for a while... before letting out a dejected sigh. And then, she lowered the sword she was holding.
"Very well. Life is precious, after all."
"Th... th... thank you very much!@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Yhead1010. Thank you, thank you!!"
The man in black continued exaggeratedly rubbing his forehead against the ground.
...It felt like I was watching the same scene over and over.
After coldly glaring at that pathetic plea for mercy, Sonozaki turned away.
"...That was amazing, Sonozaki-san."
"Th-thank goodness, Mion-san.@k@vS10/41/410200940. I'm glad you're sa..."
...Just after he muttered that,
*baaang*
Sonozaki-san's body shook as the gunshot rang out.
"Sonozaki-san...?!"
If I hadn't been running just a step behind her, I probably wouldn't have been able to catch Sonozaki-san.
I reached for the banding around her kimono sash, struggling to support her body... as her head helplessly collapsed into my shoulder.
And Sonozaki-san's bright white socks... were stained deep red.
At the same time, she coughed out a pile of blood, dying my shirt deep red.
Looking back, the man in black who should've been begging on the ground was pointing a gun straight toward Sonozaki-san.
"...Really, you're too soft.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Yhead1012. That's why you're unfit to be the next head."
"You... bastard...!"

"Life is precious...?@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Yhead1014. Heh, don't make me laugh.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Yhead1015. That weak attitude is unfit for a gangster.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Yhead1016. We live in a "kill or be killed" world."
"You... bastard..."
Sonozaki-san's face was quickly growing pale, and her breathing sounded far more fragile than earlier... It seemed like it pained her just leaving her throat.
The man in black watched her agonizing expression... and then smiled.
"Like mother, like daughter.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Yhead1018. Both falling for the same trick..."
I immediately understood what he meant by that.
It's him.
The man who carried out Mifune's orders and shot Sonozaki-san's mother, Akane-san... was standing before us right now...!
"...How dare you!"
When Otobe tried to rush out in anger, Sonozaki-san used all the strength in her body to pull him back.
"Mion-san...?!"
"...I'll settle this... on my own..."

"Keh, it's already been settled.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Yhead1020. Let the gun that killed your mother perform the last rites.@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Yhead1021. Wouldn't it be great to have something else in common with your Mama?"
The man in black once again held the gun up, with his finger on the trigger.
And the muzzle was aimed straight at her chest...!
"Demons belong beyond the gates of Hell!@k@vS10/00/VT3A_Yhead1023. ...Die, Mion Sonozaki!"
The finger holding the trigger moved.
"Aaaaah!"
A bullet burst from the gun and flew straight toward Sonozaki-san.
It was like watching in slow motion.
Sonozaki-san didn't stir. She waited there as the lead-colored bullet approached.
I could see the approaching bullet reflected in her clear eyes, under her long glossy eyelashes. And then...!
Sonozaki-san pushed herself away from my body with surprising strength, took the katana in hand, and... swung down vertically in a flash.
*ching*
I heard a dull sound.
It was like witnessing a miracle. Nobody could say a word.
...The bullet fired by the man in black... was split in two.
Then, a flash of light emanated from the sword...!
The man in black... bent backwards and fell to the ground on the spot.
Sonozaki-san approached the man in black as he lay in an unsightly pose on the ground... with her footsteps swaying as she went.
Then, she kicked his side with her toe. ...But the man in black didn't move at all.
"This sword...@k@vS10/43/410400764. I'm told that ancient warriors used this sword in battle to slice through bullets...@k@vS10/43/410400765. If this was simply a normal sword, you would've won."
He didn't respond. ...Was the man even able to hear what Sonozaki-san said? ...There was no way to know.
Otobe and I were both absolutely dumbfounded.
That's why it's called the Tamahajiki, or "orb repelling" sword...
It doesn't simply repel bullets... It slices them in half...!
Sonozaki-san noticed Otobe and I staring, and turned to each of us in turn with a reassuring smile.
But as soon as she did, Sonozaki-san's body began to sway, and then she fell to her knees.
"Sonozaki-san...?!"
But Sonozaki-san gripped the handle of her sword like a cane and used it to climb to her feet.
Then, she began walking toward the exterior hallway of the main building... No, it might be more accurate to say she advanced at a crawling pace.
The bright red blood splattered on her body left faint trails in her wake...
"Mion-san!@k@vS10/41/410200949. N-no way... it can't be...!!"
"Haah... haah..."
Never in my life had I encountered a scene that so perfectly suited the word gruesome.
Even after spilling so much blood... Sonozaki-san was still pressing on.
An ordinary person wouldn't even be conscious under these circumstances, much less still moving around...
...She was far too persistent.
Sonozaki-san was desperately trying to prevent the Sonozaki family from splitting apart and withering.
That's enough...
You gave it everything you had, and did all you could.
You can leave the rest to me.
...Inside my heart, yes, I was saying as much to her.
And yet... Sonozaki-san showed no signs of ceasing her crawl forward.
That... was her strength, her willpower.
"Mion-san!@k@vS10/41/410200951. Please... stop moving!"
Otobe cried out and clung to Sonozaki-san's back, hoping to halt her reckless advance. ...And then, he grabbed her by the hand.
"...Oto...be...@k@vS10/43/410400769. Sorry... Could I ask you... a small favor...?"
Her hands were trembling and stained red with blood.
Sonozaki-san clenched the handle of the sword in her hands, then gently held it out toward Otobe.
"With this...@k@vS10/43/410400771. With this sword...... the bell..."
"Mion-san!!!"
Her words sounded distant, but I could hear them clearly.
Everything until now was done to bring the Tamahajiki sword here. And now, she's entrusting it to Otobe.
...In other words... Sonozaki-san no longer had the strength to do it herself...
"...Mion-san!"
Sonozaki-san held the handle in her slimy, bloody hands, and firmly placed it into Otobe's hand. Then, he held his breath and listened to her speak.
"My sister... will arrive here soon.@k@vS10/43/410400774. She looks just like me... so you'll know right away...@k@vS10/43/410400775. Give her... the bell..."
"Mion-san! Please, don't say anything more!@k@vS10/41/410200956. I understand... It'll be all right...!"
"I'm sorry...@k@vS10/43/410400777. for giving you such a troubling task... in my last moments..."
"Believe me... we'll find that bell and give it to your sister!@k@vS10/41/410200958. I promise!"
"Thank you...@k@vS10/43/410400779. I have... one more favor to ask..."
Sonozaki-san's voice was getting faint and hard to hear.
Otobe brought his ear close to her lips to make sure he didn't miss anything.
...But just then,
"Oh crap, we're in deep shit!"
That didn't sound like a siren... That's not a police car!
Another wave of enemies from the Sonozaki Group!
I dashed over to the gate and closed the door just by the skin of my teeth.
...I could hear gunshots coming from the other side, but the walls were far too thick to be penetrated so easily. That ought to buy us some time.
But even so, I could hear the sound of many footsteps echoing, and someone was barking out orders. Angry, violent shouts full of murderous intent.
If we don't make it to that secret passage... they're going to kill us...!
"Arakawa-san, please go!!"
"Wh... what are you saying?!"
"I'll... go get the bell!"
Otobe clenched the bloody sword and rose to his feet.
Sonozaki-san... was no longer moving.
That fact shocked me so hard, my whole body froze for a moment... but there was no longer any time to waste on sentimentality.
"That's reckless... There's no way you can do it!@k@vS10/62/410101632. Another batch of enemies is on the way!@k@vS10/62/410101633. We need to escape as soon as..."
"Arakawa-san, please go!"
Otobe shook his head and bit his lip.
Then, he put his strong will into words that came overflowing from his lips.

"Mion-san... put her life on the line... in order to accomplish this... And when she couldn't do it... she entrusted it to me.@k@vS10/41/410200964. I... want to carry out her last request, no matter what it takes...!@k@vS10/41/410200965. Mion-san believed in me... so I have to pay her back...!"
"Otobe..."
I understood how much it pained Otobe.
For the longest time, Otobe wanted to know... why is he here? Why was he born?
They had only known each other for a single night, but humans do embrace a feeling akin to respect when another human being places their trust in them. How heavily did that weigh on Otobe?
I gazed into Otobe's eyes, then firmly nodded.
Otobe nodded back, then ran toward the back room.
Then, I turned my eyes toward the gate once more.
The harsh sound of heavy impacts against the door echoed through the room. They were likely to break through any moment now.
My senses have gotten a lot sharper since I came here.
What have I been doing in the city all this time...?
While walking through life as a writer, somewhere along the way I lost faith in my own talents.
When things didn't go well, I'd get desperate.
I managed to get by passing myself off as a popular writer... but I've worried over whether it was the right thing for me. Many times now, I've wondered if I could have another life.
However... I was shown that those sorts of worries were nothing more than calculations for living a good life.
By Sonozaki-san's... tenacity.
If all human beings are born to fulfill some purpose... then I want to serve my purpose, as well.
There's no need to search for what that is.
There's no need to hesitate, to fret over which way is right, or worry about what would be easiest.
I'll just face the things in front of me with all my strength. If my lifestyle is what enables me to do that... then it must've been fate that brought me to Hinamizawa.
Using my father's Hinamizawa interview tapes gave me a foothold that allowed me to step forward.
And thanks to the people I met here, my eyes have opened.
How can I leave the village that evoked these feelings in me without doing something to return the favor...?
I'm sure I can keep being an occult writer from now on.
But instead of just piling up hatred and resentment... I'll put the breath of the people who lived here and their ways of life into words and chronicle them on their behalf.
I'll write about Hinamizawa. And I'll find a second and third Hinamizawa out there.
I've decided...
The only reason I even had the opportunity and resolve to consider all this, was Mion Sonozaki. ...It was all you.
She entrusted us with a wish... And we're going to carry it out, even if it means putting our lives on the line!
If it's come to this, then I'll strengthen my own resolve too!!
"Otobe, wait up!!"
I started running toward the back room.
I reached the back room in no time at all.
The sliding door was wide open.
When I quietly looked inside, I saw a white-haired man dressed in a kimono, standing at the doorway with a gun in hand...
That man... must be Mifune.
And his gun was pointed... toward the floor... at Otobe?!
A wall scroll was laying on the floor, with a scabbard resting underneath.
The Tamahajiki sword was inside the scabbard, and beyond the scroll was a cupboard with its doors open wide.
And Otobe was grasping a heavy nickel bell in his hand.
(...That's the bell!)
"...Hmph."
Suddenly, Mifune noticed me and turned back.
"This is no place for amateurs.@k@vS10/00/410800028. ...If you hand it over quietly, I'll pretend I didn't see anything."
...Otobe turned toward the wall scroll instead of looking back at him, and his body was trembling.
The shiny black muzzle of Mifune's gun filled the room with a tense atmosphere, knowing that he could fire at any moment.
My pathetically quiet voice quivered in fear.
Let me have it!
But I didn't have the courage to say that.
Just... give him the bell, Otobe...
If you hand it over, your life will be spared...
If I could just say that, all this tension could be released... but I didn't have the nerve to say it.
Now, Mifune was standing in the room with his muzzle aimed directly at Otobe.
Even if I try to persuade Otobe... if I speak up now, I'll be his new target.
If that were to happen... I'd be completely at his mercy. But if not... I'd simply be an outsider.
...In other words, I can still continue being just a bystander at this stage.
It's important to be safe... and it'll all be for nothing if you die... so... just give him the bell.
Isn't it absurd to throw your life away on behalf of a woman you only just met yesterday?
You have to recognize what's important.
This isn't the place to abandon your life.
I firmly turned my eyes toward Otobe, continuing to persuade him in my mind.
Toss him the bell and run, Otobe...
Otobe slowly turned and looked at me.
Not Mifune. The person Otobe chose to face... was me.
I was probably even more surprised by Otobe's courage than Mifune was.
"Otobe..."
Can friendship blossom under any circumstances?
How long does it take to grow?
At the very least... it doesn't matter when we first met.
What matters is... how deeply he was able to impact my life.
Even people I'd known for over 10 years, I'd be able to sweep them aside.
But... Otobe... was different.
Whether or not to continue associating with Otobe wasn't the problem.
He's the ultimate coward. He couldn't even kill himself.
But... I... might really be starting to like the guy.
I couldn't just consider his lifestyle none of my business. Even I... struggled with whether to change jobs, or how I was going to live my life in the future.
Balancing the two of us on the scales of self-interest, I came to Hinamizawa looking for a convenient answer, while Otobe came here to commit suicide. Just how different were we?!
But Otobe was determined to protect the bell with his life... so I'll support him.
I've decided... I'm going to overcome my own weakness.
That's what Sonozaki-san was telling me.
The important thing isn't the end result. Or what excuses you make. Or how cool you look.
What's truly important... is to face the things you value head on...!
"...Is that so?@k@vS10/00/410800030. Then it can't be helped."
"If you want to follow that woman..."
"Graaaaaaaaahhh!"
I uttered a bizarre cry and leapt out at Mifune without hesitation!!
Surprised by my unconventional battle cry, I was able to get in close with a desperate attack and smack the gun out of Mifune's hand. It rolled down to the tatami floor.
"Otobe!@k@vS10/62/410101647. Grab that and run!!@k@vS10/62/410101648. Hurry!!"
"...You bastard!"
Mifune mercilessly punched me in the jaw.
He was far stronger than he looked. My beautiful glasses were blown right off, and the pain ran through my nose as it started to bleed.
But... my will didn't weaken. In fact, it grew stronger, so I clung to Mifune.
"Gh, graaaaahh!!"
Once I got ahold of Mifune, I sunk my teeth into the old, frail skin of his neck.
This was... no longer a struggle.
It was... a legitimate fight!
"Gwah! What is with this guy?!"
"Otobe!@k@vS10/62/410101651. Run! Ruuun!@k@vS10/62/410101652. Ruuuuuuuuun!"
"Arakawa-san...!!"
After witnessing that, Otobe finally decided to start running. However,
*bang*
In addition to the handgun by his feet, Mifune had another gun hidden in his other hand.
"Damn it... Damn it!!@k@vS10/62/410101654. Ugh?!"
*pow*
Mifune used all the strength in his body to smack me in the forehead with the gun barrel.
I felt dizzy, and the world around me grew dark.
I tried to run... but what's this...? My body can't move...
I fell down to my knees and used both my hands to support my body on the floor.
I have to keep going...
I have to... protect Otobe...
But both my arms were too weak to support my body, so I fell face down on the floor.
"Arakawa-san?!"
"Now, hand over the bell..."
Otobe stood still, clutching the bell in his hand.
Run... Oto...be...
"Arakawa-san...!!"
The moment Mifune pulled the trigger...
His whole body... shook violently.
Then, Mifune bent back and collapsed to his knees on the ground.
Standing behind Mifune... was a woman dressed in a kimono, holding a katana.
The woman who should've been dead: Mion Sonozaki.
